company_name: University of Pennsylvania Perelman School of Medicine
suggested_title: James Bauer - PhD Candidate in Neuroscience at Perelman School of Medicine
prospect_notes: James Bauer graduated from Emory University in 2020 with a degree in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology (NBB). After graduating, he spent two years as a research technician with Dr. Alan Lewis at Vanderbilt University Medical Center in Nashville, focusing on the hippocampus dentate gyrus and its role in neuropsychiatric disorders. He arrived at Penn's Perelman School of Medicine in Fall 2022 as a Neuroscience Graduate Group (NGG) PhD candidate. At Penn, he works in the Akizu Lab under Dr. Naiara Akizu, where his research focuses on neurodevelopmental disorders (NDDs) caused by missense mutations in EZH1. His work aims to explore the catalytic and phenotypic impacts of these mutations and develop EZH1-targeted therapies using human pluripotent stem cell (hPSC) models. He has published peer-reviewed work and secured NIH funding, and is now a 4th-year PhD candidate seeking to transition into an industry postdoctoral or scientist role.

_enrichment.title: PhD Candidate in Neuroscience, Perelman School of Medicine, University of Pennsylvania

company_name: University of Pennsylvania Perelman School of Medicine
suggested_title: Anna Voss - PhD Student in Neuroscience at Perelman School of Medicine
prospect_notes: Anna Voss graduated from Emory University in 2022 with a BS in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology (NBB) and a minor in Quantitative Sciences. She began her research career early, working in Dr. Mike Epstein's lab at Emory in high school studying genetic contributors to schizophrenia risk in the context of 22q11.2 deletion syndrome. During her undergraduate career she also worked with Dr. Steven Sloan studying neuron-secreted ligands hypothesized to influence astrocyte development. She joined Penn's Perelman School of Medicine Neuroscience Graduate Group (NGG) as a PhD student. Her research interests center on understanding the molecular basis of neuropsychiatric disorders. Outside of research, she is involved in rowing and rock climbing.

_enrichment.title: PhD Student in Neuroscience, Perelman School of Medicine, University of Pennsylvania

suggested_title: Hanifah Ali - MD Candidate, Yale School of Medicine Class of 2029
prospect_notes: Hanifah Ali graduated from Emory University in 2023 with a B.S. in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology (NBB), earning highest honors. During her undergraduate years at Emory, she conducted pain-related neuroscience research in the Harper PaIN Lab (Psychophysics and Imaging Neuroscience Lab) in the Department of Anesthesiology at Emory School of Medicine, where she was recognized as a Highest Honors Lab Alumna. She matriculated to Yale School of Medicine as part of the MD Class of 2029 and is placed in the Slayman Advisory House. Her research background in pain neuroscience and her strong academic record from Emory make her a compelling connection bridging Emory's pre-med pipeline and Yale medical education.

_enrichment.title: MD Candidate, Yale School of Medicine (Class of 2029)

suggested_title: Grace Hanalei Allen - MD Candidate, Yale School of Medicine Class of 2029
prospect_notes: Grace Hanalei Allen attended Emory University for her undergraduate degree and was selected to Yale School of Medicine's highly competitive MD program (Class of 2029), chosen from over 6,200 applicants. She is placed in the Slayman Advisory House at Yale School of Medicine. The Yale Class of 2029 includes 104 students representing 53 undergraduate colleges, making Emory representation notable. Her path from Emory undergrad to Yale School of Medicine reflects the strong pre-health pipeline that Emory University cultivates for its students. She is one of two confirmed Emory undergrad alumni in Yale's Class of 2029.

_enrichment.title: MD Candidate, Yale School of Medicine (Class of 2029)
